name: AWS WAF Geo-Restriction for Admin and Internal Paths
summary: Restrict access to sensitive URI paths (e.g., /admin, /internal, /ops) to an allowlisted set of countries using AWS WAF geo match and path matching logic.
objective: Reduce exposure of administrative and internal application surfaces to untrusted geographies while preserving controlled access for approved regions.
risk_mitigated: Unauthorized probing and exploitation attempts against sensitive endpoints
Credential stuffing and brute-force activity targeted at admin surfaces from non-business geographies
Operational interface exposure beyond intended regions
prerequisites: AWS WAF v2 Web ACL attached to the protected resource (ALB, API Gateway, CloudFront, etc.)
Validated country allowlist aligned to business and operational access requirements
WAF logging enabled for rollout validation
deployment_notes: Staged rollout: deploy in COUNT mode first and review logs/metrics for at least 7-14 days before enforcing.
After validation, change action from COUNT to BLOCK for the same rule and continue monitoring blocked request volume.
If needed, begin with narrower path scope (/admin only) and expand to /internal and /ops once baseline behavior is understood.
action { count {} } # switch to block {} after validation
statement { byte_match_statement { search_string = "/admin" positional_constraint = "STARTS_WITH" field_to_match { uri_path {} } text_transformation { priority = 0 type = "LOWERCASE" } } }
statement { byte_match_statement { search_string = "/internal" positional_constraint = "STARTS_WITH" field_to_match { uri_path {} } text_transformation { priority = 0 type = "LOWERCASE" } } }
statement { byte_match_statement { search_string = "/ops" positional_constraint = "STARTS_WITH" field_to_match { uri_path {} } text_transformation { priority = 0 type = "LOWERCASE" } } }
tuning_notes: Replace the sample allowlist (US, CA, GB) with your real business-country set.
Confirm URI normalization behavior in upstream components (e.g., ALB/CloudFront) and add additional patterns if encoded path variants are observed.
For machine/admin workflows traversing VPN or corporate proxies, verify apparent source geographies before enabling BLOCK.
Consider explicit allow rules for trusted admin IP sets when legitimate access may originate from non-allowlisted countries.
monitoring_guidance: Track rule metric geoRestrictSensitivePaths in CloudWatch for request count/blocked trends.
Review sampled requests and WAF logs to identify legitimate traffic from unexpected countries before enforcement.
Alert on sudden spikes in non-allowlisted access attempts to sensitive paths as possible reconnaissance or abuse signals.
false_positive_considerations: Traveling administrators may be blocked when outside allowlisted countries.
Third-party operations providers may originate from regions not yet allowlisted.
NAT/proxy egress geography may differ from expected user location.
